title: QUIC Local Harness Preflight Planning and Localhost Smoke Verification
verification_method: Local execution, artifact inspection, and report review.
preconditions: The local host can resolve and bind loopback addresses.
The test host can create temporary PEM files and qlog directories.
The managed QUIC runtime is available in the current .NET environment.
scope: Verify that the preflight planner resolves request parsing, qlog selection, endpoint resolution, and transfer-path mapping independently from the execution path, and that direct in-process localhost handshake, retry, and transfer smoke tests complete with qlog capture while IP-literal hosts still fail against a localhost-only certificate.
procedure: Run the new requirement-home test file or a narrow filter that selects `REQ_QUIC_INT_0014`.
Inspect the captured stdout, stderr, and qlog artifacts from the localhost smoke runs.
Confirm the localhost-only certificate still fails when the request host is `127.0.0.1`.
If the tree is temporarily mid-edit and a run fails for an unrelated build reason, wait briefly and rerun once to confirm the lane still behaves honestly after the transient issue clears.
expected_result: The local preflight planner is independently testable, the in-process localhost smoke lane completes for handshake, retry, and transfer with qlog artifacts, and IP-literal hostnames still fail against a localhost-only certificate without modifying the external runner repository.
status_summary: Landed; the requirement-home test lane passed under the narrow REQ_QUIC_INT_0014 filter on 2026-04-14, confirming the planner split, direct localhost handshake/retry/transfer smoke execution, qlog capture, and strict IP-literal certificate rejection.
